heading: Pyth-2 BULLS reproduction lane stabilized with equations, program core, and quarantined OCR drift
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, pyth2, bulls, cattle-problem, program-reproduction]
body: Built the next non-Q&A Pyth-2 reproduction lane around the BULLS program and its preceding equation block. `pyth2_bulls_program.py` now normalizes the three stable bulls equations (`W1 = Y1 + 5/6 X1`, `X1 = Y1 + 9/20 Z1`, `Z1 = Y1 + 13/42 W1`), reconstructs the base program core, and emits `pyth2_bulls_program_artifacts.json`. The stable outputs are now explicit: the reconstructed program core scanning `Y1` and `X1` candidates, the linear printed family with first rows `(742,534,297,1580/3)`, `(1484,1068,594,3160/3)`, and `(2226,1602,891,1580)`, and the answer row as the third family member with integer `Z1 = 1580`. The lane also isolates the remaining OCR drift instead of promoting it: the damaged line-30 scan/STEP-6 optimization wording and the TAB print-layout formatting stay quarantined as source-layer ambiguities. Prior-art refs were attached for RT, chromogeometry, Ptolemy/UHG, and the Egyptian-fractions/HAT line because this block explicitly sits on fractional modular structure before the later ellipse reinterpretation.
